D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Von Kries Adaptation

slide the illuminant from daylight to tungsten and watch adaptation discount the colour cast

The visual system rescales each cone channel to the prevailing light, so a white wall reads white whether lit by noon sky or a warm bulb — chromatic adaptation.

Slide the illuminant warmth. The top swatches are the raw light reaching the eye — drenched in the lamp's colour. The bottom swatches apply the Bradford transform back to daylight: the cast lifts and the colours hold.
